75161.subject: Action Required: Your Gemini API service paused due to spend caps
75161.body:
Increase or remove your spend cap to resume Gemini API service.

Google AI  
Studio<https://c.gle/AOPyDKTh8inbrn2XfwSIIrNhhHw-TmomhYKOhpiFuvRgtF7IAZdyPz6WkUHD8Whuaiex_l-k5Jtm7q1wFWlxxSRwSrHayUF_4-WOuWkrjjs-jZp3mDPqwkma3gYEOtHxPSFhIik7JGafx-amOkcb1bsCe4rV>

Gemini API service paused due to your spend cap

Your project 878341164164 has reached its spend cap for this month, and  
your Gemini API service has been paused as a result. To resume your Gemini  
API service, you need to increase or remove the spend cap. If no action is  
taken, usage against your monthly spend cap will reset on the 1st of the  
month.

Increase my spend  
cap<https://c.gle/AOPyDKR5uB5NWGY-WSvRoy8XW2wKgWKaozauc8GbYTQUNBUQdbMGcwnLmwdVbq8wCwygNsgr_f8y28TlsTZErV2jGp9G6-nR6T-NhrPcrASvJwBRsSIpMid889AusQ1g4vcbyI9F9vlxWKwIsgrJUpZUYjC6j7u0YhU>

About project spend caps

Project caps are designed to protect your account from unexpected overages.  
If you move a project to a different billing account, any spend cap you  
already set for that project will persist, but any accumulated spend will  
reset to $0 for the new billing cycle.

Learn  
more<https://c.gle/AOPyDKQ-5XwJ2yfvkAr_1F4LkidEU_D9ZEiHw9Q3o2daOW1NtaNhB9jopq1n9rFmcQwqenP3yqZUD59S2OxF5gXgVwDx4jFKaFspmvid6CI5u_Dxjt73hWibVJyLJSKj1svCKKAJy76fL8SMCMuV5xRInhwlyQSawsgzzHfT0EufdObA6MIv-6FNDMfEg8sNbA>

Other recommended actions

For latency-tolerant workloads like CRM updates or regression tests,  
consider using the Batch API or Flex tier to reduce overall costs in the  
future.

Explore  
optimizations<https://c.gle/AOPyDKRbOxefUwisn9S33iwqTLKJQ399UL80scoK53bBQHNFZ6Vkm2F4dZpL1dk88ogZZw20-4fVdo7W_APUW4ci9HzXccsnnP4dn6s5vU-dXFQywkR-AcO7tbuvOU1XY_SQuvPH-K_BPiN9guCQprZ4Wo7vTsLk8wOhahrcRhkMhU8y5JMK3rvDLY3K-g>

75160.subject: Is child care becoming more affordable?
75160.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjB9AAcAAAAAA0b0qzGSF5zmR890lupTGIyDLgsiCLIg03A0_TdAD5rGIgq8r8bYXajDfpvGvLRLu187cqP_5yGvgNcgO9N-n7qlK60V_F1ZB-hiZi5VSWk 

June 27th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjB9AAcAAAAAA0b0qzGTe47Du5os16eHoqJw1RNaqI40MqvcmCMvQPwv5TMgqmxzg1m_b93yeKpup9Z2ooyvrVOwJAUhOuMOMnoaby7FXkwCTKrGzRXd5Ck 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjB9AAcAAAAAA0b0qzGUn23i9CT2QQEM3wNvimvURlPr4bAJZ2ZaPjwj2c-aFJaKLsSqQN_H3e7htS2bWFs0C_Rxd1lPLbhwbtfePEfnnfaHLNSqbH15sFU 



 
  


Anna Kerr

Senior audience editor



 People all over the world say that they cannot afford to have their desired number of children because of the soaring cost of child care. But where is it most unaffordable? We compared costs with earnings in 36 countries and found 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjB9AAcAAAAAA0b0qzGVyp9hUBxb28Kv7Bn7UUrnbVSIQR3SZbxjOUmKfucuhfGWzWn-mJYFgBDi_NdkJWAsglyUxFk4dO5K8Pmn9IX22YrfaMWLkA 
some surprising results .
 


 If you’re craving an escape from the dilemmas of real life, pick up one of 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MjB9AAcAAAAAA0b0qzGWT6hTQcdVyKaX904JjKU6JHblui21Hx2SEC1lVnA8d3TeRYMqfjd8ap-BLhvvobLM50GZO6Afsc39BRoJjKD8tpmBEqM7zg 
our recommended novels . 
 Many explore family secrets.

75159.subject: OpenAI's First Chip, Crusoe’s Deal With Meta, and SambaNova's 5x Leap.
75159.body:
Pre-IPO Briefing

StartEngine Private (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/MVTKG-_vljwW77MY1K5ltdm3W99f7mw5QQ2DQMVmxVR3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3llW2LjKG514CxfNN8C5Yytsv69ZW97ryP078y2v9W3BhYWw1JdhG_W1bXn4l4Cx78xVD6lTF2FLDf-W4SWFll8qvY6RW1ZWs2w7LWX18W1HHlSS4fL9q8W9cl_5r5HCvMyW3tcNGW29dKkJW2Kwj9j2Lh-7nW9dNbQR56QYtcN5kDd0jL6Q6YVHkdBT6tPtV0VB1MBQ86TX6vVb0dSG1cLJ9JW3G_kL535SQtvW7rNk5g2zrrcNMXVs5DDj5qhW86w4fM2lGY3JW1MtDFx8ZZVP9f3k95xx04 )

Pre-IPO Briefing (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/MVTKG-_vljwW77MY1K5ltdm3W99f7mw5QQ2DQMVmxVR3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3llW2LjKG514CxfNN8C5Yytsv69ZW97ryP078y2v9W3BhYWw1JdhG_W1bXn4l4Cx78xVD6lTF2FLDf-W4SWFll8qvY6RW1ZWs2w7LWX18W1HHlSS4fL9q8W9cl_5r5HCvMyW3tcNGW29dKkJW2Kwj9j2Lh-7nW9dNbQR56QYtcN5kDd0jL6Q6YVHkdBT6tPtV0VB1MBQ86TX6vVb0dSG1cLJ9JW3G_kL535SQtvW7rNk5g2zrrcNMXVs5DDj5qhW86w4fM2lGY3JW1MtDFx8ZZVP9f3k95xx04 )

Hi Oleg,

Custom silicon, gigawatts of compute, and a few eye-catching numbers.

There's a lot in this week's Pre-IPO Briefing, so let's get right to it:

- Crusoe signed a new AI computing deal with Meta. The agreement will provide about 1.6 gigawatts (GW) of capacity. For context, 1 GW ‌can power roughly 750K U.S. homes.¹
- OpenAI unveiled its first custom chip in partnership with Broadcom. The company also told major brands at Cannes Lions that it expects to have a $100B ad business by 2030.²
- Ripple made an equity investment in Flutterwave, part of a round with an estimated $3.2B valuation. The companies will work to expand financial services across Africa.³
- SambaNova’s executive chairman said the company is set to raise up to $1B at an estimated $10B valuation. The AI chip startup was last valued at an estimated $2B in February.⁴
- Superhuman acquired AI detection startup GPTZero as it continues building a comprehensive productivity suite. GPTZero had 19M users and $30M in ARR.⁵
- Kalshi is in talks to raise a new round at an estimated $40B valuation, which could close as soon as Q3. The company last raised at an estimated $22B valuation in May.⁶

These reports don’t imply specific results for investors with exposure to these companies via StartEngine Private. Past performance is not indicative of future results. Additionally, there's no guarantee that revenue, funding, or valuation will continue to grow for these companies, or that they’ll be profitable.

Regulation of prediction markets is a hotly contested political and legal issue. Kalshi has been the subject of state and federal scrutiny. Future legal or regulatory developments could impact Kalshi’s operations.

Not long ago, exposure to companies like these was reserved for VCs, institutions, or whoever was simply in the right room.

This week is a reminder of how much value gets created before a company ever goes public, and how good it feels to be early.

Every day, StartEngine (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/MVTKG-_vljwW77MY1K5ltdm3W99f7mw5QQ2DQMVmxVR3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3

75157.subject: 📰 AI-curated Daily Crypto Digest — June 27
75157.body:
AI-curated Daily Crypto Digest

Автор: CryptoBeholder.com


Автор: CryptoBeholder.com

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-june-27-cryptobeholder-dusxf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BKqL9%2F1hKQXKVf3cpC%2Ftkug%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1N42Z-WYZ3lsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqwl0df4~s6-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqwl0df4-s6
  
🧠 AI's Crypto News Brief: Risk-off dominates as Bitcoin clings to $60K, ETFs hemorrhage $1.79B and Strategy slips below its own Bitcoin value. Yet institutions keep building — Tether, Chainlink and Securitize all pushed deeper into traditional finance. 📉 Bitcoin ETFs bleed $1.79B in second-largest weekly outflow US spot Bitcoin ETFs shed roughly $1.79 billion last week, their second-heaviest outflow since launch, as Bitcoin briefly slid near $58,000 before steadying around $60,000. BlackRock's IBIT led the exit, leaving the average holder down close to 40%. Read full story 🏦 Strategy's market value sinks below its Bitcoin stash Michael Saylor's Strategy is now worth less than the Bitcoin it holds, with its mNAV ratio dropping under 1 for the first time this cycle. The vanished premium removes the easy share-issuance engine that funded fresh BTC buys, while its STRC preferred shares trade well below par. More details 🇪🇺 Binance loses MiCA license as Coinbase and OKX court its EU users After Binance failed to secure a MiCA license covering its roughly 450 million EU users, rivals pounced. Coinbase's Brian Armstrong and OKX's Star Xu launched sign-up bonuses worth up to 8% of deposits to pull defecting traders onto their regulated platforms. Read more 🪙 Tether unlocks lending against its $23B gold pile Tether is extending its tokenized gold strategy, letting XAUT holders borrow against their bullion without selling the underlying metal. The bullion-backed loans mirror Bitcoin-backed lending and put the issuer's sizable gold reserves to work. See details 🛡️ Polymarket hack grows to $3.1M as senators demand a CFTC probe Polymarket revised its breach loss upward to $3.1 million and pledged full refunds, but the pressure intensified. Senators John Curtis and Adam Schiff urged the CFTC to investigate the platform over reportedly deceptive marketing practices. Read full story 🔗 Chainlink lands DTCC and Pangea for TradFi settlement Chainlink's Runtime Environment was selected by DTCC for collateral management and by Pangea for FX settlement, pushing blockchain deeper into core financial plumbing. The wins landed alongside two of Chainlink's strongest network-growth days of 2026. More details 🚀 Securitize heads to public markets with $400M in hand Tokenization firm Securitize expects about $400 million on its upcoming sto
